title: Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup

seo.title: How to Make Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup — Easy Mexican Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup. This easy Mexican recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Mexican Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup — a beloved Mexican classic

description: Pozole Rojo is a hearty and traditional Mexican soup, famous for its rich red broth and tender hominy. It is often prepared for special occasions but is comforting any day. This robust dish is packed with flavor from chiles and pork, creating a truly satisfying culinary experience.

instructions.title: Cook the Pork
instructions.text: Place the pork shoulder cubes in a large pot or Dutch oven. Add half of the onion, 3 unpeeled garlic cloves, and 1 teaspoon of salt. Cover with 8 cups of water or broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the pork is very tender. Skim off any foam that rises to the surface. Remove pork, shred, and set aside. Strain the cooking liquid and reserve.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chiles
instructions.text: While the pork cooks, toast the guajillo and ancho chiles in a dry skillet over medium heat for 1-2 minutes per side until fragrant but not burnt. Remove from heat and place in a bowl. Cover with hot water and let them rehydrate for 20-30 minutes until soft.

instructions.title: Make the Chile Sauce
instructions.text: Drain the rehydrated chiles. In a blender, combine the chiles with the remaining 3 unpeeled garlic cloves, the other half of the onion, 1 teaspoon of Mexican oregano, 1/2 teaspoon of ground cumin, and 1 cup of the reserved pork cooking liquid. Blend until very smooth. Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, pressing on the solids to extract all liquid. Discard the solids.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer
instructions.text: In the same large pot, add the strained chile sauce and bring to a simmer over medium heat for 5 minutes. Add the shredded pork, drained hominy, bay leaves, and the remaining reserved pork cooking liquid (or add more broth/water if needed, aiming for about 4 cups total liquid for the final soup). Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for at least 30 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.

instructions.title: Adjust Seasoning
instructions.text: Taste the pozole and adjust salt as needed. Remove the bay leaves before serving. The soup should be rich and flavorful.

instructions.title: Serve with Garnishes
instructions.text: Ladle the hot pozole into individual bowls. Serve immediately with a variety of garnishes on the side, allowing everyone to customize their bowl. Common garnishes include shredded cabbage, thinly sliced radishes, lime wedges, dried oregano, and tostadas or tortilla chips for dipping.

tips: For a deeper flavor, you can lightly sear the pork cubes before adding water to cook them. This adds a layer of richness.
Always strain your chile sauce. This step removes tough skins and seeds, ensuring a smooth, restaurant-quality broth. Do not skip it!
Pozole tastes even better the next day as the flavors continue to develop. It stores well in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days or can be frozen for longer.
article: Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup is a beloved Mexican Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
